scientific_question: Can the slow Agent turn a candidate-selection regret owned by one retrieved capability Skill into a safe single-surface Skill-body PATCH that is supported on new development seeds?

triggering_evidence.mechanism_diagnosis: All four held-out targets retrieved the learned Skill and supplied an effect-distinct repair_level_shift candidate, but two still selected identity. This is a scoped selection gap, not a retrieval or candidate-supply gap.

seed_provenance.selection: These were the three remaining severe level-shift seeds whose public effective view matched the Skill during the outcome-blind Run 4 held-out screening.
seed_provenance.not_heldout_claim: Run 5 is development data. These seeds cannot be used as future held-out evidence.

attribution_contract.multiple_or_no_owning_capabilities: SELECTION_MISS remains routed to global candidate_policy.selection_guidance.
attribution_contract.reason: Prefer the narrowest causally implicated deployable surface and avoid making all defect families globally more aggressive.

success_gate.minimum_evidence: At least one new development case exposes selection regret after retrieving level_shift_contrast_candidate.
success_gate.edit_channel: A controller-authorized PATCH enters paired replay and receives a non-INCONCLUSIVE verdict.
success_gate.growth: The predicted selection behavior changes, target recovery and median gain pass frozen rules, all automatic risk and scope checks pass, and the PATCH is promoted.

anti_leakage_and_stop_rules: Do not modify H1 using the four completed held-out target outcomes and then retest those targets as held-out evidence.
Do not change grader thresholds, probe doses, injection parameters, or risk selection rules.
If no development case exhibits selection regret, report that fact and do not manufacture a PATCH.
Any H2 promotion requires a separately preregistered held-out test on entirely new seeds.
